title: Boat Rental in Cook Islands: a Day on the Water Guide 2026
html:
Exploring the turquoise lagoons of the Cook Islands by boat is the quintessential tropical experience, and you can book your voyage through BoatHire24 for a day from around EUR 150. Departing from Avarua Harbour on Rarotonga, expect to pay between EUR 150 and EUR 2,500 depending on the vessel and duration, with options for every budget.

Where to set off in Cook Islands

Avarua Harbour on Rarotonga is the main hub for yacht charters and day trips. The smaller Avatiu Harbour serves cargo and some private boats, while social events often start from the Rarotonga Sailing Club. To explore remote motus, head to Aitutaki where the lagoon is dotted with sand islets such as Tapuaetai (One Foot Island) and Maina Island. The tiny settlement of Arutanga on Aitutaki provides a relaxed base for chartered excursions.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cook Islands?

Prices for boat rental in Cook Islands start from EUR 150 for a half-day shared tour and rise to EUR 2,500 for a crewed luxury yacht. A typical half-day motorboat for up to six people costs around EUR 400, while a full-day catamaran charter for eight guests averages EUR 1,200.

  • Shared lagoon tour: EUR 150 per person, half-day, includes snorkelling stops by Aitutaki Lagoon.
  • Small motorboat (half-day, private, up to 6 guests): EUR 400, ideal for fishing or island hopping.
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 10 guests): EUR 1,200, perfect for sunset cruises and coral reef views.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full-day, up to 12 guests): EUR 2,500, with a skipper and hostess included.

You can compare these options and book on the skippered charters in Cook Islands page, which lists verified vessels and transparent pricing.

Which boat to rent in Cook Islands?

  1. Motor yacht, fast, comfortable for day trips to Aitutaki, up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,500. For a luxury option, see the Princess F70 charter page.
  2. Catamaran, stable and spacious for families, up to 10 guests, from EUR 800 half-day.
  3. Sailing yacht, classic cruising at a slower pace, up to 8 guests, from EUR 600 half-day.
  4. RIB (rigid inflatable boat), nimble for snorkelling and fishing, up to 6 guests, from EUR 300 half-day.

When is the best season to rent a boat?

The best season is the dry winter from May to October, when the south-east trade winds (the kona) blow consistently. These months offer calm seas and sunny weather, with average water temperatures of 26°C. The wet season from November to April can bring heavy rain and cyclone risks, though many boats operate year-round with flexible cancellation policies.

During August, the winds shift to a north-westerly pattern, making the lee side of Rarotonga the most sheltered departure point for a day charter.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 150, EUR 2,500 per charter
  • Peak months: July, August, September
  • Trip length: 4, 8 hours typical
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests (most common)
  • Currency: EUR (with local payment in NZD)

Questions about boat rental in Cook Islands

Do I need a boating licence to rent a boat in Cook Islands?

You do not need a licence if you hire a skipper, which is standard for all charters on BoatHire24; the operator will confirm requirements for bareboat rental, which are rare.

Can I visit Aitutaki from Rarotonga as a day trip by boat?

It is possible but the 220-nautical-mile journey takes over six hours one way, so most visitors fly to Aitutaki and charter locally.

What is included in the charter price?

Most charters include fuel, a skipper, snorkelling gear, and refreshments; confirm your booking on BoatHire24 for precise inclusions.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cook Islands or list your own. Whether you are planning a sunset cruise from Avarua or a lagoon tour on Aitutaki, the platform connects you with verified operators. If you own a vessel, you can become a host and earn income from your boat in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cook Islands and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
